folds.reason: Same maternal uncle of Náṣiri'd-Dín Sháh / governor of Zanján; appositive 'the Majdu’d-Dawlih' vs run-on Majdu’d-Dawlih are surface variants.
Identical name; only difference is '-i-' nisba connector vs space.
Same Grand Vazír of Muḥammad Sháh; appositive 'the Qá’im-Maqám' vs '-i-Qá’im-Maqám' suffix are surface variants (the rule's exact example).
Same Imám Ḥasan, son of ‘Alí and Fáṭimih; difference is only the leading article 'the'.
Same once-influential siyyid whose turban was burned and who was led begging door to door; 'Áqá' is an added honorific prefix.
GPB/DB: 'Mírzá ‘Alí, whose courage earned the surname ‘Alíy-i-Sardár'; bare form drops the Mírzá honorific.
Same disciple sent to introduce the Faith in India; bare list form drops the Shayḵh honorific.
Same leading mujtahid of Sárí who confined Quddús; '-i-Sárí' nisba vs 'mujtahid of Sárí' appositive are surface variants.
Identical; only curly vs straight apostrophe in the nisba. Both the sole survivor of the Míyámay company.
Same Sang-Sar martyr; bare form drops the Mullá honorific.

folds.keeper: Áqá Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí, son of Siyyid Aḥmad
merged: Mírzá Muḥammad-‘Alí, son of Siyyid Aḥmad
folds.reason: Same youth at the fort entrance (hand carried away / head blown off), father of Áqá Siyyid Ḥusayn 'the beloved'; 'Áqá' is an added honorific prefix.

merged: ‘Abdu’lláh Ḵhán, brother of Ḥájí Muṣṭafá Ḵhán
folds.reason: Same brother of Ḥájí Muṣṭafá Khán; second form adds the Khán title.

folds.keeper: Mary Leonora Woulfe Sheil, Lady Sheil
folds.reason: Same author of 'Glimpses of Life and Manners in Persia'; reordered name forms (the rule's Lady Sheil example).
